verdicts.note: Evidence confirms this is Linear's developer platform.
Evidence URLs confirm developers.linear.app is the correct developer site.
Linear is clearly a productivity/project management tool based on evidence describing issues, projects, teams, cycles.

The record claims an 'official hosted MCP server' but the evidence URL https://mcp.linear.app/mcp does not appear in any of the provided evidence documents. The evidence only shows GraphQL API, SDK, OAuth2, and personal API keys—no mention of MCP server.
Evidence confirms both personal API keys (Authorization: <API_KEY>, no Bearer prefix) and OAuth2 (Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>).
Pricing page confirms 'API and webhook access' on Free plan; GraphQL docs confirm personal API keys created in Security & Access settings with no approval needed.
Free plan shows '2 teams' and '250 issues' limit; personal API keys and OAuth app registration are self-serve per evidence.
Evidence confirms GraphQL endpoint at api.linear.app/graphql, supports introspection, covers issues/projects/teams/comments/files/webhooks, TypeScript SDK available, explorable via Apollo Studio.

No evidence of a Model Context Protocol server in any of the provided documents. The URL https://mcp.linear.app/mcp is not mentioned anywhere. Evidence only discusses GraphQL API, SDK, OAuth, and webhooks.
GraphQL API is accessible via self-serve personal API keys or OAuth2, free plan available, comprehensive docs provided—buildable now.
No blockers evident; API is accessible on free plan.
All evidence URLs are present and support the claims about GraphQL, auth, SDK, pricing, and API/webhook availability.
Free plan limit (2 teams/250 issues) is confirmed in pricing page. However, the flag about 'Official MCP server is centrally hosted by Linear' is not supported by evidence.

revised.name: Linear
revised.one_liner: Linear offers a GraphQL API with personal API keys or OAuth2 for building integrations and agents, available self-serve on the Free plan.

auth_methods.notes: Personal API keys created in Security & Access settings; passed via 'Authorization: <API_KEY>' header (no Bearer prefix).
For apps built for others; standard OAuth2 flow, access token passed as 'Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>'.

revised.self_serve_notes: API and webhook access is available on the Free plan (limited to 2 teams / 250 issues); personal API keys can be generated instantly from account settings with no approval process. OAuth app registration is also self-serve via developer console.

api_surface.notes: Single GraphQL endpoint (api.linear.app/graphql) covering issues, projects, teams, comments, file storage, webhooks, and agent interactions. Official TypeScript SDK wraps the schema. Supports introspection and is explorable via Apollo Studio.

evidence.supports: GraphQL endpoint, personal API key and OAuth2 authentication details, SDK reference
Overview of developer resources including Authentication, Agents, TypeScript SDK, and Agent Interaction Guidelines
Free plan includes API and webhook access
API and webhooks documentation confirming availability across plans

flags: Free plan API access limited to 2 teams/250 issues, may require paid plan for larger workspaces
